title: Postoperative canine orthopedic pain escalation
presenting_complaint: Poor pain control 24 hours after tibial plateau leveling osteotomy (TPLO)
history: Elective TPLO performed yesterday morning for complete cranial cruciate ligament rupture
Intraoperative analgesia: epidural morphine/bupivacaine, fentanyl CRI intraoperatively
Current postoperative protocol: buprenorphine 0.02 mg/kg IV q6h, carprofen 4.4 mg/kg PO q24h started this morning
Patient reluctant to bear any weight on operated limb
Vocalizing intermittently, especially on patient handling
physical_exam: Respiratory rate: 36 breaths/min — elevated, panting pattern
Marked discomfort on manipulation and palpation of operated limb
Surgical incision: mild swelling, no discharge, wound edges intact
Contralateral limb: mild muscle guarding noted on exam
diagnostics_available: Post-operative radiographs: implant in expected position, no osteotomy gap, no hardware failure

decision_nodes.description: Determine whether presentation represents expected postoperative pain versus a complication requiring investigation
Evaluate adequacy of current analgesic protocol and identify gaps
Design a multimodal escalation strategy appropriate for this patient
Define a monitoring and reassessment plan with clear decision thresholds

disallowed_shortcuts: Jumping to IV opioid infusion without first reassessing current protocol
Dismissing pain as expected without considering complication

model_answer_framework.summary: Candidate should recognize uncontrolled postoperative pain, rule out surgical complication through targeted reassessment, then escalate to a multimodal analgesic strategy including an opioid with higher efficacy (e.g., methadone or hydromorphone), consider NSAID continuation, add adjuncts (gabapentin, lidocaine or ketamine CRI), define reassessment checkpoints, and provide appropriate safety monitoring.
